_purpose: Five boundary-pushing game-MASHUP concepts from the catalog-mashup-ideation workflow (18 concepts -> critiqued -> 5 distinct winners). Each makes a library's SIGNATURE CAPABILITY the core mechanic in a way none of the existing 7 lib-lane flagships do: sensory-substitution audio-as-a-sense, cellular-automata material sim, deformable-graph tower-defense, soft-body-as-player, and real-geo-data rail traversal. All offline, single-file, mobile-friendly, no NEW vendored lib (verlet implemented inline; faults embedded inline). Build ladder order: powdermind -> force-web -> rope-runner -> echo-cartographer -> tectonic.

entries.goal: Build 'POWDERMIND', a falling-sand cellular-automata RESCUE PUZZLE using real p5.js loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). The CORE MECHANIC is a per-frame cellular-automata material grid simulated on a p5 pixel buffer (loadPixels/updatePixels): SAND falls and piles at an angle of repose, WATER flows and seeks its level, FIRE spreads to OIL and WOOD and is extinguished by water, OIL floats on water, and WATER+FIRE produces rising STEAM. The player does NOT steer an avatar - you control MATTER with a radial brush (drag to deposit, with an erase mode). The puzzle is plumbing the emergent physics: build a sand dam to divert a lava flow, drop water to create a steam updraft that lifts a floating 'emberling' creature up to the exit, or ignite a line of oil as a fuse to burn through a wood wall blocking the path. Each level grants a FIXED BUDGET of each material (the score-attack constraint) and a spreading-fire TIMER (the pressure). OBJECTIVE: guide every emberling to the exit before the fire or timer runs out. WIN = all emberlings saved; LOSE = an emberling burns or the timer expires. SCORE = emberlings_saved*500 + leftover_budget + speed_bonus; best score per level to localStorage, plus a daily-seeded grid for a shared bragging score. ESCALATION across levels: acid that eats walls, electrified water, more emberlings, tighter budgets. PERFORMANCE IS CRITICAL (this is the make-or-break): use a typed Uint8 material array, scan bottom-up, and use ACTIVE-CELL / dirty-rectangle stepping (only simulate cells near recent activity) so it holds 60fps on mobile - do NOT naively scan the whole grid every frame. JUICY: combustion particle bursts, hiss/whoosh WebAudio for steam and a rising fire-roar as flames spread, a satisfying 'plink' when an emberling reaches the exit, screen-shake on big ignitions. FAIR OPENING (survival-arena rule): level 1 is a calm no-timer sandbox tutorial so the player learns the material language before any pressure. Mobile-first: large brush, on-screen material-picker buttons + erase toggle, touch-friendly. Vibe: cozy-but-tense, warm ember palette (charcoal bg, amber sand, teal water, orange fire, soft glowing emberlings). Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'FORCE WEB DEFENSE', a tower-defense game where the maze is a LIVE deformable graph, using real D3 v7 (load via <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/d3/7.8.5/d3.min.js">, auto-vendored offline) for a force-directed simulation. The CORE MECHANIC is d3-forceSimulation: dozens of NODES joined by spring EDGES under charge/link/center forces. Your CORE is a position-FIXED center node (fx/fy pinned). Enemy RAIDER nodes spawn at the rim and crawl inward ALONG edges toward the core - they can only travel where a link exists, advancing each tick along the shortest current path (compute a per-tick Dijkstra/BFS path-to-core for raider routing). You defend by EDITING THE GRAPH in real time: SWIPE across an edge to CUT it (reroutes raiders the long way and re-tensions the whole web), or DRAG from one node to another to FORGE a new link (re-tensions everything, nodes spring to new positions). Tap a charged TURRET node to blast nearby raiders outward along the web; you place a few turret nodes on a budget. The catch: cut too many edges and you FRAGMENT the graph, orphaning turrets and creating a death spiral. OBJECTIVE: survive escalating waves; WIN = endure as many waves as possible, LOSE = a raider reaches the core. SCORE = waves_survived*100 + raiders_destroyed; best wave to localStorage. LEGIBILITY IS THE KEY RISK - tune for READABILITY: keep the core position-fixed, use HIGH alphaDecay and velocityDecay so the web settles FAST and stays damped (no perpetual nervous jiggle), draw edges as clean lines and raiders as bright dots flowing along them, and DISAMBIGUATE TOUCH clearly (a tap/short-swipe NEAR an edge = cut; a drag STARTING ON a node = forge). FAIR OPENING (survival-arena rule): wave 1 is a handful of slow raiders on a simple star graph so the player learns cut vs forge before the swarm arrives. JUICY: a satisfying 'snap' + recoil when an edge is cut and the web re-tensions, a zap + particle burst on turret fire, screen-shake when a raider is destroyed, rising tension drone as raider density climbs. Mobile-first, touch-driven, responsive SVG/canvas that fits any screen. Vibe: neon cyber-web on near-black (#0a0e14), cyan edges, hot-magenta raiders, gold turrets. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'ROPE RUNNER', an endless soft-body physics survival platformer where the PLAYER IS A WOBBLING JELLY BLOB, using real p5.js loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline) for rendering. IMPLEMENT THE PHYSICS INLINE with a small VERLET mass-spring integrator (no external physics library) so the game is fully self-contained and offline. The player is a SOFT BODY: a ring of ~12 verlet point-masses joined by springs (plus a few cross-springs / a pressure term for volume) - a genuine jiggling jelly-creature, not a rigid circle. The SIGNATURE MECHANIC is squash-and-stretch momentum: tap-and-HOLD to INFLATE pressure (the blob tenses round and bouncy) and RELEASE to relax (it splats flat and oozes under a low gap); flick left/right to roll. Master the physics to survive: pre-tense on impact to BOUNCE off a spike floor, grab a swinging verlet ROPE (an anchored point-mass chain with distance constraints) and let the elastic recoil SLINGSHOT you over a pit, or go limp to squeeze through a slot narrower than your resting diameter. Hazards close in (crushing walls, sweeping lasers, pendulum mauls) and the run accelerates. OBJECTIVE: survive as far as possible; distance = score, plus STYLE points for clean squeezes and no-touch slingshots; best distance to localStorage. LOSE = crushed or fall off-screen. CONTROL LEGIBILITY IS THE MAKE-OR-BREAK (do NOT let it feel soupy): use HIGH spring stiffness with strong damping, a FIXED physics timestep with a clamped number of constraint-relaxation iterations per frame, and make the squeeze-through-gap check DETERMINISTIC (test the blob's current bounding-span against the gap width, not chaotic per-point overlap) so deaths feel FAIR (survival-arena rule). FAIR OPENING: the first ~10 seconds are slow with wide gaps and gentle bounces so the player learns inflate/deflate/roll before the crush speeds up. JUICY: the blob overshoots and settles on every bounce, a stretchy 'boing' WebAudio note scaled by impact, a satisfying elastic 'twang' on a rope slingshot, particle splat on death, screen-shake on crushes. Mobile-first: hold-anywhere to inflate, on-screen left/right + grab buttons. Vibe: bright, gummy, candy palette - a translucent glowing jelly on a clean gradient, neon hazards. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'ECHO CARTOGRAPHER', a sensory-substitution exploration game where you navigate a pitch-black 3D cavern BY SOUND, using the REAL three.js library (load via a normal <script src> CDN tag, e.g. https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/build/three.min.js, auto-vendored offline; do NOT hand-roll WebGL) for the 3D scene + raycasting, and the REAL Tone.js (load via <script src> CDN tag) for the echo audio. The scene is rendered ALMOST FULLY BLACK - the player cannot see the geometry. Move first-person with WASD + mouse-look on desktop and a virtual joystick + drag-look on mobile. The CORE MECHANIC is ECHOLOCATION: tap PING and (a) a Tone.js percussive synth fires a pulse AND (b) a fan of ~24 three.js raycasts shoots from the camera. Each ray HIT briefly lights a small fading sprite-dot at the impact point for ~1.8s (the ping momentarily 'photographs' that part of the room) AND feeds that hit's distance+angle into a POOLED (pre-allocated, mobile-safe) Tone echo voice: echo DELAY proportional to hit distance (distance/speed-of-sound), Tone.Reverb wet scaled by the mean hit distance (a proxy for how enclosed the space is), and stereo PAN by the hit's azimuth. So a near wall = a tight bright click; a far cavern = a long booming wash. CRITICAL SAFETY CUE: a ray that returns NO hit within max range = a CHASM/drop in that direction and MUST be unmistakable - play a distinct hollow 'wind' tone for missing rays, never just silence. A limited PING BATTERY that recharges slowly forces navigating BY EAR between pings. OBJECTIVE: find 3 glowing crystals (each emits a faint constant locator chime that grows louder as you approach) and reach the exit before your AIR runs out. WIN = all crystals + exit; LOSE = air depleted or you walk off a chasm. SCORE = crystals*1000 + time bonus; best 'depth reached' to localStorage. ESCALATION: bigger mazes, fewer pings, then a single slow STALKER you must ping to locate (its footstep audio is your other warning), then time pressure. FAIR OPENING (survival-arena rule): level 1 is a tiny 3-room box with UNLIMITED pings so the player learns the audio language safely. NOTE on the 3D render gate: the camera + a WebGL context must initialize cleanly even though the scene is dark (keep a faint ambient floor grid or skybox so a WebGL context is always present). Mobile-first, touch joystick + PING button. Vibe: tense, beautiful-in-the-dark - the fading echo-dots are the only light, deep blue-black, crystals glow cyan. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'TECTONIC: PLATE RIDER', a geo-action survival game where you ride Earth's fault lines, using globe.gl loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline) for the 3D globe, and the REAL Tone.js (load via <script src> CDN tag) for the rumble audio. EMBED A SIMPLIFIED SET of major tectonic fault-line polylines INLINE as arrays of [lat,lng] points (no external dataset) - include several recognizable real faults: the Pacific 'Ring of Fire', the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, the San Andreas, the Himalayan front, the East African Rift. Draw them on the globe as glowing seams (globe.gl pathsData) over a solid ocean-blue globeMaterial (NO external earth texture - atmosphere glow ON). V1 DE-RISK: at the start the player PICKS ONE fault (this choice IS the difficulty dial - the mid-ocean ridge is the gentle lane, the Ring of Fire is the high-score-multiplier suicide lane). Their research sub is a globe.gl point that rides along that SINGLE non-branching fault as an arc-length RAIL: steering moves the sub forward/back along the chosen polyline (interpolate position by arc length) and the camera (Globe.pointOfView) eases to follow. The CORE LOOP: stress builds at random points ahead ON YOUR FAULT, telegraphed by a swelling ripple (globe.gl ringsData) from a pending epicenter; race to it and TAP to plant a sensor before it ruptures. Tag in time = points + a green 'mapped' ripple. Rupture untagged = a red shockwave ring races back down the fault toward you - be off that segment or lose a sensor. Tone.js renders a deep sub-bass rumble that pitches UP as the nearest local stress peaks, so you HEAR the rupture about to fire (the dread cue). REAL GEOLOGY DRIVES DIFFICULTY: convergent/subduction segments build stress faster and bigger than transform segments. OBJECTIVE: survival score; WIN-framing = highest 'quakes mapped before the big one' + a 'seismographer rank'; LOSE = run out of sensors. SCORE + best rank to localStorage. ESCALATION: faster stress, multiple armed epicenters at once, climbing magnitude (bigger/faster shockwaves). FAIR OPENING (survival-arena rule): the picked-fault choice is the difficulty - present the calm ridge as the tutorial lane. JUICY: rising rumble + screen-shake as a rupture nears, a confident chime + particle burst on a last-second tag, the globe spins fast on the 'play again' loop. Mobile-first portrait layout, drag-to-spin to inspect, big on-screen steer + TAG buttons. Vibe: dark space backdrop, neon-mint mapped / coral rupture, clean rounded HUD with a radial stress meter. Complete and playable on first load.
